DESCRIPTION:
Test verifies that an alert appears with 'User does not exist.'
    using dynamic event waiting instead of static timeouts.
NAME: Negative: Error message for non-existent user

DESCRIPTION:
Scenario:
    1. Call the API with invalid ID (triggering 404)
    2. Check that the logging system records the attempt as 'Failed'
    3. Verify the status is saved in the DB
NAME: Negative: Ensure API errors are logged to DB

DESCRIPTION:
Business Flow:
    1. Login with valid credentials using LoginPage
    2. Select product and handle the 'Product added' dialog
    3. Verify product presence in the CartPage
    4. Complete checkout via CheckoutPage
    5. Verify success and perform logout
NAME: E2E: Full Checkout Flow (Login to Purchase)

DESCRIPTION:
Standard positive scenario:
    1. HTTP Status 201
    2. Header Content-Type is JSON
    3. Response Body types validation
NAME: Positive: Create resource and verify data contract

DESCRIPTION:
Scenario:
    1. Call the API to create a resource (POST)
    2. Save the operation result into SQLite database (INSERT)
    3. Verify the record exists in the DB (SELECT)
    4. Cleanup is handled automatically by the db_client fixture (Teardown)
NAME: Database: Log API results to SQL table and Verify

DESCRIPTION:
Negative scenario:
    - Sending 'None' and invalid types
    - Verifying server stability and error handling
NAME: Negative: Create resource with invalid data types

DESCRIPTION:
Test Case: Sending data types that violate the API contract.
    Goal: Verify how the backend handles unexpected input (arrays/numbers instead of strings)
NAME: Negative: Handling Malformed Data (Type Mismatch)

DESCRIPTION:
Test collects all links from the homepage and verifies them
    using parallel API HEAD requests for maximum speed.
NAME: Negative: High-speed broken links check

Negative: Validate empty checkout form (No-Wait Flow)
